explanation: The specification requires the controller to enter fail-safe mode if the external ADC fails to initialize or reports an error for 5 or more retries during start-up. In terms of atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' must be true (controller is in start-up), and the triggering condition is either 'ADCConnFailure' (external ADC failed to initialize) or 'ADCError' (ADC reports error), but this must coincide with 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' (5 or more attempts made). The property should require that whenever the conjunction of being in Start-up Mode and either failure or error with sufficient retries occurs, a transition to failSafeMode is mandated. Template 41 matches this requirement: it expresses that globally, whenever in StartUpMode and the combined ADC error condition occurs, then next failSafeMode must be true (the controller transitions to fail-safe mode on the next step). For N_DURATION, any value >0 suffices; we use 1 to match one-step transition.

The natural language specifies that in Start-up Mode, if the external ADC fails to initialize or reports an error condition after 5 retries, the controller must enter fail-safe mode. The proposition 'StartUpMode' denotes being in start-up mode, 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' captures the failure/retry threshold, and 'failSafeMode' indicates entry into fail-safe mode. The template chosen expresses that "Globally, if we are in Start-up Mode and the ADC has exceeded its retry limit, then the controller must (immediately) enter fail-safe mode." This direct implication is best captured by template 41: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) → X bool_exp3). Here, bool_exp1 is StartUpMode, bool_exp2 is ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5, and bool_exp3 is failSafeMode. We set N_DURATION to null as it is not used in this template.

The requirement states that if during Start-up Mode, the external ADC fails to initialize or reports an error condition five or more times (i.e., after a maximum of 5 retries), then the controller must transition to the fail-safe mode. In the provided atomic propositions, 'StartUpMode' signals the controller is in start-up, 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' indicates that 5 or more ADC failures have occurred, and 'failSafeMode' denotes the machine has entered fail-safe mode. To formalize this, the property must express that in StartUpMode, if ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 ever holds, then, inevitably, failSafeMode will also hold at some point (i.e., the system must enter fail-safe mode). Therefore, we use the LTL template: G((StartUpMode & ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> F failSafeMode), which means 'Globally, if in StartUpMode and 5 or more ADC failures occurred, then eventually failSafeMode will occur.' This directly implements the intent of the requirement.

The requirement states that in Start-up Mode, if the external ADC either fails to initialize (ADCConnFailure) or reports an error (ADCError) repeatedly for a maximum of 5 retries (ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5), the controller must transition to fail-safe mode (failSafeMode). This is best captured by the template 'Globally, if StartUpMode and the ADC fails condition (either connection failure or error) and the number of retries is at least 5, then failSafeMode must hold.' This is represented as G((StartUpMode & (ADCConnFailure | ADCError) & ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> failSafeMode). This ensures that at all times in Start-up Mode, if the ADC failure/error with enough retries occurs, failSafeMode is entered as required.

The requirement specifies that if, after 5 or more retries, the external ADC has failed to initialize or has reported an error, then the controller must transition to (enter) fail-safe mode. The atomic propositions 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5', 'ADCConnFailure', and 'ADCError' are relevant: 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' indicates the retry limit was reached, while 'ADCConnFailure' or 'ADCError' represent ADC initialization failure and error state. Therefore, the logical condition describing the antecedent is ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 & (ADCConnFailure | ADCError). The consequent is 'failSafeMode', indicating the controller has entered fail-safe mode. The most appropriate LTL template is 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', which states: Globally, if the condition (retry threshold reached and failure or error occurred) holds, then the controller must enter fail-safe mode at that time. This fully captures the intent of the requirement.

The expressions are as follows:
bool_exp1: ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 & (ADCConnFailure | ADCError) (the ADC failed repeatedly and failed to initialize or produced an error)
bool_exp3: failSafeMode (the controller is in fail-safe mode)
Other bool_expX not used.
N_DURATION not needed for this template.

This property ensures that whenever the Start-up Mode is active, if the external ADC fails to initialize or reports an error condition after a maximum of 5 retries (as indicated by 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5'), then the controller will enter fail-safe mode (as indicated by 'failSafeMode'). The template uses bool_exp1 = StartUpMode to represent being in start-up mode, and bool_exp3 = (ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 -> failSafeMode), expressing that the triggering condition is when the retry limit is reached, at which point fail-safe must follow. This translation captures the user requirement that, during start-up, the controller must transition to fail-safe upon ADC initialization or error condition retried 5 times.

This LTL property states that whenever the system is in StartUpMode and the condition occurs where either the external ADC fails to initialize (ADCConnFailure) or the ADC reports an error (ADCError) for 5 or more retries (ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5), then eventually the controller must enter the failSafeMode. Translation: 'If the external ADC fails to initialize or reports an error condition after a fixed number of retries (maximum 5), the controller shall transition to the fail-safe mode.' is mapped to: 'If in StartUpMode and ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5, eventually failSafeMode.' The property is encoded as '(G ((StartUpMode & ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> (F[0,1] failSafeMode)))' with N_DURATION=1, capturing an immediate or next-step fail-safe transition requirement after error detection. The atomic proposition ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5 captures both 'ADC fails to initialize or reports error condition' and 'retries >= 5.'

The requirement is that if, during start-up, the external ADC fails to initialize or reports an error for 5 or more retries, the controller must transition to the fail-safe mode. Among the available atomic propositions, 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' represents this condition directly, while 'failSafeMode' indicates the controller is in fail-safe mode. The best template to capture this is 'G (bool_exp1 -> bool_exp3)', i.e., globally, whenever we are in StartUpMode and ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5, failSafeMode must be entered. Here, 'bool_exp1' is 'StartUpMode & ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5', and 'bool_exp3' is 'failSafeMode'. A fixed N_DURATION is not needed because this is about an instantaneous condition during start-up.

The natural language states: If (in Start-up Mode) the external ADC fails to initialize or reports an error condition after a fixed number of retries (maximum 5), the controller shall transition to fail-safe mode. 

In terms of atomic propositions:
- 'StartUpMode' indicates the controller is currently in Start-up Mode.
- 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' signals that the ADC has failed for 5 or more retries (capturing the 'after a fixed number of retries' part).
- 'failSafeMode' means the controller has entered fail-safe mode.

LTL choice:
In every state, if we are in StartUpMode and either the ADC has failed to initialize for 5+ retries or reported an error for the 5th time, then failSafeMode shall follow. This is a classic safety (response) property, and can be written as:
G(((StartUpMode & ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> failSafeMode))
This ensures that as soon as we are in StartUpMode and the retry/error threshold is surpassed, failSafeMode is entered. 
We choose template 40: G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> bool_exp3)
bool_exp1 = StartUpMode
bool_exp2 = ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5
bool_exp3 = failSafeMode
No other boolean expressions are necessary. N_DURATION is not used.

This template captures the requirement that, **whenever** the given condition occurs (in Start-up Mode and ADC failure threshold reached), failSafeMode becomes true (or at least, must be true from/at that moment).

The requirement states that in Start-up Mode, if the ADC fails to initialize or reports an error after a maximum of 5 retries, the controller must transition to fail-safe mode. Mapping the atomic propositions: 'StartUpMode' indicates the controller is in Start-up Mode. 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' captures the ADC failure or error after 5 or more retries. 'failSafeMode' means the controller is in fail-safe mode. The LTL property should express: whenever in Start-up Mode, if the ADC fails (5 retries), then eventually (within 1 time step) fail-safe mode is entered. The most precise template is:

G ( (StartUpMode & ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5) -> (X failSafeMode))

This captures that whenever both 'StartUpMode' and 'ADCRetries_greaterThanOrEqual_5' hold, in the next step 'failSafeMode' must hold, matching the requirement that the controller enter fail-safe mode after 5 failed initialization attempts during startup.
